L1.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists in the API's tenant isolation mechanism. The server trusts a client-controlled HTTP header to determine whether a request should skip tenant-scoped authorization checks. By setting this header, a low-privileged authenticated user can bypass permission validation and tenant scoping, enabling cross-tenant data access, exposure of sensitive user credential fields through nested entity relations, and ultimately full account takeover via leaked password reset tokens.

ground_truth.reason: The server trusts a client-controlled HTTP header to bypass authorization and tenant isolation checks, allowing any authenticated user to access other tenants' data, leak sensitive credential fields, and take over accounts.
